stem: Let \( f(x,y) = [[A]]x^{2} + [[B]]xy + [[C]]y^{2} + [[P]]x + [[Q]]y \). The point \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \) is a critical point of \( f \). What is the second-derivative test discriminant \( D = f_{xx}f_{yy} - (f_{xy})^{2} \) at this critical point?
solution: For this quadratic, \( f_{xx}=2\cdot[[A]] \), \( f_{yy}=2\cdot[[C]] \), and \( f_{xy}=[[B]] \) everywhere (the Hessian of a quadratic form is constant), so \( D = (2\cdot[[A]])(2\cdot[[C]]) - [[B]]^{2} = [[answer]] \) at \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \) (or any point).
distractor_rationales: This adds \( (f_{xy})^{2} \) instead of subtracting it.
This forgets the factor of 2 in both \( f_{xx} \) and \( f_{yy}\).
This sums \( f_{xx} \) and \( f_{yy} \) instead of multiplying them.
This forgets to square \( f_{xy} \).
This is off by four from the correct value.
This is off by four from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x,y) = [[A]]x^{3} + [[c1]]x + [[B]]y^{3} + [[c2]]y \). The point \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \) is a critical point of \( f \). What is the second-derivative test discriminant \( D = f_{xx}f_{yy} - (f_{xy})^{2} \) at this critical point?
solution: Since \( f(x,y) \) separates as a sum of a function of \( x \) alone and a function of \( y \) alone, the mixed partial \( f_{xy}=0 \) everywhere. Here \( f_{xx}=6\cdot[[A]]x \) and \( f_{yy}=6\cdot[[B]]y \), so at \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \): \( D = (6\cdot[[A]]\cdot[[x0]])(6\cdot[[B]]\cdot[[y0]]) - 0^{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This sums \( f_{xx} \) and \( f_{yy}\) instead of multiplying them.
This mistakenly assumes a nonzero mixed partial \( f_{xy} \), even though \( f \) is additively separable.
This forgets the factor of 6 in both \( f_{xx}\) and \( f_{yy}\).
This has the wrong overall sign.
This is off by six from the correct value.
This is off by six from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x,y) = [[A]]x^{2}y^{2} + [[P]]x + [[Q]]y \). The point \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \) is a critical point of \( f \). What is the second-derivative test discriminant \( D = f_{xx}f_{yy} - (f_{xy})^{2} \) at this critical point?
distractor_rationales: This drops the \( (f_{xy})^{2} \) term entirely, forgetting the mixed partial is nonzero here.
This sums \( f_{xx}\) and \( f_{yy}\) instead of multiplying them.
This uses \( f_{xy}=2\cdot[[A]]xy \) instead of the correct \( f_{xy}=4\cdot[[A]]xy \), missing a factor of 2.
This swaps the sign, computing \( (f_{xy})^{2} - f_{xx}f_{yy} \) instead of \( f_{xx}f_{yy}-(f_{xy})^{2} \).
This is off by four from the correct value.
This is off by four from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x,y) = [[A]]\sin(x)y^{2} + [[P]]x \). The point \( (0,[[y0]]) \) is a critical point of \( f \). What is the second-derivative test discriminant \( D = f_{xx}f_{yy} - (f_{xy})^{2} \) at this critical point?
distractor_rationales: This mistakenly uses \( \sin(0) \) instead of \( \cos(0) \) in the mixed partial \( f_{xy} \), making it vanish incorrectly.
This drops the negative sign, reporting \( (f_{xy})^{2} \) instead of \( -\,(f_{xy})^{2} \).
This forgets to square the mixed partial.
This reports \( 2f_{xy} \) instead of \( -(f_{xy})^{2} \).
This is off by four from the correct value.
This is off by four from the correct value.
